accepted.question_text: Determine the exact asymptotic growth of D(n), where D(n) is the minimum number of distinct Euclidean distances determined by an n-point set in the plane.

evidence.quote: Let D(n) = min|P|=n D(P); that is, D(n) is the minimum number of distinct distances that a set of n points in R2 can determine.
Problem 1. Find the exact asymptotic value of D(n).

accepted.question_text: Characterize the planar n-point sets P with D(P) = O(n/sqrt(log n)), meaning sets that span a near-optimal number of distinct distances up to the currently best known order of magnitude.

evidence.quote: Since the asymptotic value of D(n) is still unknown, we consider sets P of n points in R2 that satisfy D(P) = O(n/sqrt(log n)), and refer to such sets as near-optimal.
Problem 2. Characterize the near-optimal point sets.

accepted.question_text: Prove or disprove that, for some sufficiently small epsilon > 0, every near-optimal planar n-point set contains at least Omega(n^epsilon) points on a single line.

evidence.quote: Since this also appears to be quite difficult, Erdos asked whether there exists a line with Omega(n^epsilon) points of the set. Embarrassingly, even this weaker variant remains open.
Problem 3. (Erdos [21]) Prove or disprove: For a sufficiently small epsilon > 0, every near-optimal point set contains Omega(n^epsilon) points on a common line.

accepted.question_text: Prove or disprove that for every near-optimal planar n-point set P and every epsilon > 0, every constant-degree algebraic curve contains at most O(n^(0.5+epsilon)) points of P.

evidence.quote: By combining these three results, we obtain that for every near-optimal set P of n points, every constant-degree algebraic curve contains O(n^(43/52)) points of P.
Problem 4. Prove or disprove: For every near-optimal set P of n points and epsilon > 0, every constant-degree curve contains O(n^(0.5+epsilon)) points of P.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dcurve(n), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by n points lying on a constant-degree algebraic curve in the plane when the curve contains neither a line nor a circle.

evidence.quote: We denote by Dcurve(n) the minimum number of distinct distances spanned by n points on a constant-degree curve gamma, when gamma does not contain any lines or circles.
Problem 5. Find the asymptotic value of Dcurve(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the exact value of Dno3l(n), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set with no three collinear points.

evidence.quote: Denote by Dno3l(n) the minimum number of distinct distances determined by a set of n points, no three of which are collinear.
Problem 6. Find the exact value of Dno3l(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the exact value of Dhat_conv(n), where Dhat_conv(n) is the largest number such that every set of n points in strict convex position contains a point whose distances to the other n-1 points realize at least Dhat_conv(n) distinct values.

evidence.quote: Let Dhat_conv(n) denote the maximum number satisfying that for any set P of n points in convex position, there exists a point p in P such that there are at least Dhat_conv(n) distinct distances between P and P \ {p}.
Problem 7. Find the exact value of Dhat_conv(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dgen(n), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in general position, meaning no three points are collinear and no four points are cocircular.

evidence.quote: We say that a set of points is in general position if no three points are collinear and no four points are cocircular. Denote by Dgen(n) the minimum number of distinct distances determined by a set of n points in general position.
Problem 8. Find the asymptotic value of Dgen(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dpara(n), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in general position that determines no parallelograms.

evidence.quote: This led to denoting by Dpara(n) the minimum number of distinct distances determined by a set of n points in general position that do not determine any parallelograms.
Problem 9. Find the asymptotic value of Dpara(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of D_d(n), where D_d(n) is the minimum number of distinct distances that an n-point set in R^d can determine.

evidence.quote: Denote by Dd(n) the minimum number of distinct distances that a set of n points in Rd can determine.
Problem 10. Find the asymptotic value of Dd(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of D3^o(n), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by n points constrained to lie on a sphere in R^3.
accepted.context_brief: Points on a sphere in R3

evidence.quote: Let Do_d(n) denote the minimum number of distinct distances that a set of n points on a hypersphere in Rd can determine. Tao observed that the bound from [31] remains valid when the point set is on a sphere in R3.
Problem 11. Find the asymptotic value of Do_3(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Do_d(n) for d >= 4, where Do_d(n) is the minimum number of distinct distances determined by n points on a hypersphere in R^d.

evidence.quote: Let Do_d(n) denote the minimum number of distinct distances that a set of n points on a hypersphere in Rd can determine.
Problem 12. Find the asymptotic value of Do_d(n) for d >= 4.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dcurve^(d)(n), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by n points on a constant-degree curve in R^d that does not contain an algebraic helix.

evidence.quote: We denote by Dcurve^(d)(n) the minimum number of distinct distances that are determined by n points on constant-degree curve in Rd that does not contain an algebraic helix.
Problem 13. Find the asymptotic value of Dcurve^(d)(n).

accepted.question_text: Derive non-trivial lower or upper bounds for distinct distances determined by points constrained to algebraic varieties in R^d for dimensions d >= 3.

evidence.quote: It is natural to ask what happens when the points are restricted to a surface or to an algebraic variety of any dimension.
Problem 14. Derive non-trivial distinct distances bounds for points on varieties in Rd, when d >= 3.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of D(m,n), the minimum number of distinct distances determined between two planar point sets of sizes m and n when only cross-set distances are counted.

evidence.quote: Let D(m, n) denote minimum number of distinct distances that are determined by point sets in R2 of respective sizes m and n. That is, D(m, n) = min D(P1,P2).
Problem 15. Find the asymptotic value of D(m, n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dlines(m,n), the minimum number of distinct distances between m points on one line and n points on another planar line when the two lines are neither parallel nor orthogonal.

evidence.quote: Purdy conjectured that when the lines are neither parallel nor orthogonal, the number of distinct distances should be superlinear. We denote as Dlines(m, n) the minimum number of distinct distances in such a scenario.
Problem 16. Find the asymptotic value of Dlines(m, n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dcurves(m,n), the minimum number of distinct distances between m points on one constant-degree algebraic curve and n points on another when the curves avoid the exceptional cases of containing parallel lines, orthogonal lines, or concentric circles.

evidence.quote: We denote by Dcurves(m, n) the minimum number of distinct distances that can occur when gamma1 and gamma2 do not contain parallel lines, orthogonal lines, and concentric circles.
Problem 17. Find the asymptotic value of Dcurves(m, n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dline(m,n), the minimum number of distinct distances between a set of m points on a line and a set of n unrestricted points in the plane.
accepted.context_brief: One collinear set and one free set

evidence.quote: We denote by Dline(m, n) the minimum number of distinct distances between a set of m points on a line and a set of n unrestricted points, both in R2.
Problem 18. Find the asymptotic value of Dline(m, n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dline(m,n) under the extra assumption that every line parallel or orthogonal to the given line contains only O(1) points of the unrestricted set.

evidence.quote: Bruner and Sharir studied this problem with the extra restriction that every line parallel or orthogonal to l contains O(1) points of P2.
Problem 19. Find the asymptotic value of Dline(m, n) when also assuming that every line parallel or orthogonal to the line contains O(1) points of P2.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of D(3,n) when the three distinguished points are not collinear, where D(3,n) asks for the minimum number of distances determined between those three points and another set of n points.
accepted.context_brief: Three noncollinear points versus n points

evidence.quote: Another bipartite problem with unrestricted point sets involves D(3, n). That is, we wish to find the minimum number of distances between n points and three points.
Problem 20. Find the asymptotic value of D(3, n) when the three points are not collinear.

accepted.question_text: Derive non-trivial bounds for bipartite distinct-distance problems in R^d for d >= 3.

evidence.quote: Bipartite bounds can also be obtained in various other scenarios in higher dimensions. It is not yet clear what the main problems and difficulties are, so we conclude this section with a deliberately vague problem.
Problem 21. Derive non-trivial bounds for bipartite distinct distances problems in Rd, for d >= 3.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of subset(n), the largest number such that every n-point set in the plane contains a subset of that size in which no positive distance occurs more than once.
accepted.context_brief: Largest distinct-distance subset in the plane

evidence.quote: Let subset(n) = min|P|=n subset(P). In other words, subset(n) is the maximum number satisfying that every set of n points in R2 contains a subset of subset(n) points that do not span any distance more than once.
Problem 22. (Erdos [19, 26, 28]) Find the asymptotic value of subset(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of subset(L) when L is a sqrt(n) by sqrt(n) integer lattice, where subset(L) is the size of the largest subset of lattice points that spans each positive distance at most once.
accepted.context_brief: Distinct-distance subset in the planar lattice

evidence.quote: Erdos and Guy considered the following special case of Problem 22.
Problem 23. (Erdos and Guy [26]) Find the asymptotic value of subset(L), where L is a sqrt(n) x sqrt(n) integer lattice.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of subset(L_d) when L_d is the d-dimensional integer lattice n^(1/d) x ... x n^(1/d), where subset(L_d) is the size of the largest subset whose positive distances are all unique.

evidence.quote: That is, they considered a d-dimensional lattice Ld of the form n^(1/d) x ... x n^(1/d).
Problem 24. (Erdos and Guy [26]) Find the asymptotic value of subset(Ld), where Ld is an n^(1/d) x ... x n^(1/d) integer lattice.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of subset_d(n) for d >= 3, where subset_d(n) is the largest number such that every n-point set in R^d contains a subset of that size in which each positive distance occurs at most once.

evidence.quote: Let subsetd(n) denote the maximum number satisfying that every set of n points in Rd contains a subset of subsetd(n) points that do not span any distance more than once.
Problem 25. Find the asymptotic value of subsetd(n) for d >= 3.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of subset'(n), the largest number such that every n-point planar set contains a subset of that size which spans no isosceles triangles.

evidence.quote: Let subset'(n) denote the maximum number satisfying the property that every set of n points in the plane contains a subset of subset'(n) points that do not span any isosceles triangles.
Problem 26. (Brass, Moser, and Pach [6]) Find the asymptotic value of subset'(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of phi(n,3,2), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in which every three points determine at least two distinct distances, equivalently a set with no equilateral triangles.

evidence.quote: The value of phi(n, 3, 2) is the minimum number of distinct distances determined by a set of n points that do not span any equilateral triangles.
Problem 27. Find the asymptotic value of phi(n, 3, 2).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of phi(n,3,3), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in which every three points determine three distinct distances, equivalently a set with no isosceles triangles, including degenerate collinear ones.

evidence.quote: The value of phi(n, 3, 3) is the minimum number of distinct distances determined by a set of n points that do not span any isosceles triangles. Here and in the following cases we also consider degenerate polygons, such as a degenerate isosceles triangle whose three vertices are collinear.
Problem 28. Find the asymptotic value of phi(n, 3, 3).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of phi(n,4,3), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in which every four points determine at least three distinct distances, equivalently a set with no squares.

evidence.quote: The value of phi(n, 4, 3) is the minimum number of distinct distances determined by a set of n points that do not span any squares.
Problem 29. Find the asymptotic value of phi(n, 4, 3).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of phi(n,4,4), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in which every four-point subset determines at least four distinct distances; equivalently, the set contains no rhombus, no rectangle, and no deltoid with one vertex equidistant from the other three.

evidence.quote: The value of phi(n, 4, 4) is the minimum number of distinct distances determined by a set of n points that do not span any rhombuses, rectangles, or deltoids with one vertex that is equidistant to the three other three.
Problem 30. Find the asymptotic value of phi(n, 4, 4).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of phi(n,4,5), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in which every four-point subset determines at least five distinct distances.

evidence.quote: Not much is known about the case of phi(n, 4, 5), even though it is considered to be one of the main variants of the problem.
Problem 31. Find the asymptotic value of phi(n, 4, 5).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of phi(n,5,9), the minimum number of distinct distances determined by an n-point planar set in which every five-point subset determines at least nine distinct distances.

evidence.quote: We do not go over the various cases of k = 5, but only mention one that is considered interesting. Erdos asked whether phi(n, 5, 9) = Omega(n^2).
Problem 32. Find the asymptotic value of phi(n, 5, 9).

accepted.question_text: Find stronger general lower bounds for phi(n,k,l), the minimum number of distinct distances forced in an n-point planar set when every k-point subset determines at least l distinct distances.

evidence.quote: For positive integers k, l, we consider planar point sets where every k points determine at least l distinct distances. Let phi(n, k, l) denote the minimum number of distinct distances that are span by such a set of n points.
Problem 33. Find stronger general bounds for phi(n, k, l).

accepted.question_text: Prove or disprove that if a planar n-point set P spans only O(n/sqrt(log n)) distinct distances, then the additive energy E(P) = |{(a,b,c,d) in P^4 : a+b = c+d}| must be large.

evidence.quote: The additive energy of A is defined as E(A) = |{(a, b, c, d) in A^4 : a + b = c + d}|.
Problem 34. Prove or disprove: If a set P of n points in R2 spans O(n/sqrt(log n)) distinct distances, then E(P) is large.

accepted.question_text: For a finite set A in R, determine the asymptotic size of the difference set A-A under the assumption that the Cartesian product A x A spans few distinct distances in the plane.

evidence.quote: For a finite set A in R, Hanson's results were pushed further by Roche-Newton and by Pohoata. In particular, Pohoata proved that if a cartesian product A x A spans a small number of distinct distances, then the difference set A - A cannot be too large.
Problem 35. For a finite set A in R, find the asymptotic value of |A - A| when A x A spans few distinct distances.

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of Dhat(n), the largest function such that every n-point planar set contains a point incident to at least Dhat(n) distinct distances to the other points.

evidence.quote: Let Dhat(n) be the minimum value such that for every set P of n points in R2 there exists a point p in P satisfying D({p}, P \ {p}) >= Dhat(n).
Problem 36. Find the asymptotic value of Dhat(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of DhatSigma(n), where DhatSigma(n) is the minimum, over all planar n-point sets P, of the sum over p in P of the number of distinct distances from p to the rest of P.

evidence.quote: Given a planar point set P and a point p in P, we denote by Dhat_p(P) the number of distinct distances between p and the other points of P. We set DhatSigma(P) = sum_p Dhat_p(P) and DhatSigma(n) = min|P|=n DhatSigma(P).
Problem 37. Find the asymptotic value of DhatSigma(n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of L(m,n), the minimum number of distinct point-to-line distances determined by a set of m points and a set of n lines in the plane.

evidence.quote: Denote by L(m, n) the minimum number of distinct distances between a set of m points and a set of n lines, both in R2 (where the distance between a point and a line is defined in the usual way).
Problem 38. Find the asymptotic value of L(m, n).

accepted.question_text: Find the asymptotic value of vgen(n), the minimum number of distinct vectors spanned by an n-point planar set in general position, meaning no three points are collinear and no four are cocircular.

evidence.quote: Erdos, Furedi, Pach, and Ruzsa studied the case of distinct vectors for point sets in general position (that is, no three points on a line and no four points on a circle). We set vgen(n) = min|P|=n v(P), where the sum is taken over every set of n points in general position.
Problem 39. Find the asymptotic value of vgen(n).

trace.notes: Mapped a 20-page survey paper titled Distinct Distances: Open Problems and Current Bounds. The explicit open-problem regions are the numbered Problem 1 through Problem 39 statements across the introduction and Sections 2 through 9.
Extracted Problem 1 from the introduction and treated the page-2 summary bullets as duplicates of later numbered problems.
Extracted Problems 2 through 4 from the section on the structure of planar sets with few distinct distances.
Extracted Problems 5 through 9 from the section on restricted planar point sets.
Extracted Problems 10 through 14 from the higher-dimensional section.
Extracted Problems 15 through 21 from the bipartite-distances section.
Extracted Problems 22 through 26 from the section on subsets with no repeated distances.
Extracted Problems 27 through 33 from the section on local distance properties.
Extracted Problems 34 and 35 from the additive-combinatorics section.
Extracted Problems 36 through 39 from the final miscellaneous section.
Rewrote each numbered problem into a standalone question by inlining the notation or geometric setting stated immediately around the source problem statement and avoiding unsupported additions.
Kept the 39 numbered problems as canonical items and merged only introductory duplicates into their later numbered formulations.
All accepted items passed self-containedness, evidence coverage, complete-sentence evidence, no-new-facts, and schema checks.
Saved exact JSON output to outputs/runs/1406.1949.json and updated outputs/latest.json.
